what: Declarative framework for batch and streaming ETL pipelines.
note: Existing DLT code still runs; classic SKUs kept the DLT prefix. Databricks docs increasingly shorthand this to "Lakeflow pipelines"; the open-source engine is "Spark Declarative Pipelines".

what: Orchestration for multi-task jobs and pipelines.
note: Folded into the Lakeflow umbrella alongside Connect and Declarative Pipelines. The sidebar now groups it with pipelines under "Jobs & Pipelines".

what: SQL-optimized compute that powers Databricks SQL.
note: Databricks docs date this to 2023; some write-ups say 2022.

what: In-workspace Git client for version-controlling notebooks and files.
note: Git folders support asset types Repos didn't and require a remote repo URL.

what: In-workspace UI for browsing and managing Unity Catalog securables — schemas, tables, models, functions, and other assets.
note: Renamed to reflect that it manages all Unity Catalog securables, not just data. Shows as "Catalog" in the sidebar.

what: AI-assisted dashboards for visualizing and sharing query results.
note: The user-facing product is "AI/BI Dashboards"; the underlying API still uses the "Lakeview" name. Distinct from the older SQL/Redash "legacy dashboards".

what: Infrastructure-as-code format for defining and deploying Databricks resources (jobs, pipelines, etc.) as a versioned bundle.
note: Docs read "Declarative Automation Bundles (formerly known as Databricks Asset Bundles)"; the DABs acronym was kept. Supersedes the deprecated dbx deployment tool.

what: Curated, governed conversational agents scoped to a business domain, now able to take autonomous multi-step actions.
note: Renamed in early July 2026 to reflect the shift from conversational query spaces to autonomous agents. Shows as "Genie Agents" in the sidebar.

what: Business-user-facing UI giving non-technical users a single conversational entry point to data and AI.
occasion: Renamed to Genie (April 2026), then Genie One (June 2026)
note: Docs state "Genie One was previously known as Databricks One." Announced as Databricks One at Data + AI Summit (June 2025; Public Preview September 2025), renamed to Genie in April 2026, then to Genie One in June 2026 — the /one URL was kept for compatibility. Genie One folds in all Databricks One capabilities plus account-level access and native mobile apps.

what: Managed search over Unity Catalog data; now supports full-text and hybrid search without pre-computed embeddings.
note: Renamed at GA on 2026-06-25 as the service expanded beyond pure vector search. The "Mosaic AI" prefix came from the 2023 MosaicML acquisition branding.

what: The serverless data warehouse for running SQL queries, dashboards, and BI on the lakehouse.
note: The May 26, 2021 release notes state "SQL Analytics is renamed to Databricks SQL"; the access entitlement became "Databricks SQL access" (formerly "SQL Analytics access"). Distinct from the SQL Warehouse compute that powers it.

what: The open table format that adds ACID transactions, schema enforcement, and time travel to data lakes.
note: The proprietary "Databricks Delta" feature was open-sourced as Delta Lake on April 24, 2019 and donated to the Linux Foundation; the "Databricks Delta" name fell out of use. Same storage layer, new (and now OSS) name.

what: Agent Bricks capability that orchestrates multiple specialized agents, routing each request to the right one.
note: Docs describe it as "Supervisor Agent (formerly Multi-Agent Supervisor, MAS)"; the doc URL still ends in /multi-agent-supervisor. Shortened as the Agent Bricks multi-agent framework reached GA.

what: AI coding assistant that generates and fixes SQL and Python across notebooks, the SQL editor, and pipelines, grounded in Unity Catalog metadata.
note: The Databricks Assistant docs now live under "Get coding help from Genie Code" at the same /notebooks/code-assistant page. Databricks' launch blog frames Genie Code as the newest member of the Genie family rather than an explicit rename, but the docs continuity and community coverage ("Databricks Assistant is now Genie Code") treat it as one.

name: dbx
what: Databricks Labs CLI that templated and deployed jobs via the Jobs API.
note: Databricks recommends Asset Bundles (now Declarative Automation Bundles) instead; the dbx docs are archived and there's an official "Migrate from dbx to bundles" guide. Different config format (.dbx/project.json vs databricks.yml) — a replacement, not a rename.

name: Legacy dashboards
what: The original Redash-based SQL dashboards in Databricks SQL.
note: Superseded by AI/BI Dashboards. A dismissable warning began November 3, 2025; direct access to legacy dashboards and their APIs ended January 12, 2026, with an in-place upgrade tool available until March 2, 2026.

name: Legacy Databricks CLI
what: The original Python-based command-line interface (versions 0.18 and below).
note: Databricks does no support or feature work on the legacy CLI and recommends the new Go-based CLI (0.205+); the legacy docs are archived. Same core concept, different implementation — but a rewrite, not a straight rename.

name: DBFS mounts
what: Mounting cloud object storage into the DBFS namespace under /mnt.
note: Mounts bypass Unity Catalog governance; Databricks recommends UC volumes, external locations, or workspace files instead, and provisions new workspaces without mount access. Deprecation date is approximate — docs state the guidance without a single cutover date.

what: Highly available, low-latency service for deploying ML and GenAI models behind autoscaling REST endpoints.
note: The March 2023 release notes state "Model Serving, formerly Serverless Real-Time Inference, is now generally available." Later carried the "Mosaic AI" prefix in some surfaces.

name: Workspace Model Registry
what: The legacy per-workspace MLflow model registry, including model stages (None/Staging/Production/Archived).
note: Databricks recommends Models in Unity Catalog for governing and deploying models. Stages are not supported in Unity Catalog — the three-level namespace plus aliases (e.g. @champion) replace them; migrate versions with copy_model_version(). A replacement, not a rename: different governance model and API.

name: Workspace Feature Store
what: The legacy per-workspace feature store, with its own UI and metadata layer separate from Unity Catalog.
note: Available only for workspaces created before August 19, 2024 (16:00 UTC). Databricks recommends Feature Engineering in Unity Catalog, which unifies feature tables with Delta tables and UC lineage; the databricks-feature-store package moved to databricks-feature-engineering.

name: Liquid Clustering
what: Delta Lake data-layout optimization that supersedes Z-ordering and Hive-style partitioning; clustering keys can evolve without rewriting existing data.
occasion: GA on Databricks Runtime 15.4 LTS and above
note: Databricks recommends liquid clustering for all new tables — over both Z-ordering and partitioning. Existing ZORDER BY columns can be reused directly as clustering keys.

name: Unity Catalog Volumes
what: Unity Catalog objects that govern non-tabular data (files of any format) alongside tables — the recommended replacement for DBFS mounts.
note: Managed volumes use Databricks-managed storage; external volumes govern existing cloud storage. The overview doc no longer restates a single GA date; Volumes reached GA in late 2023. Pairs with the DBFS mounts deprecation.

name: Lakehouse Federation
what: Query external databases (Postgres, MySQL, Snowflake, Redshift, and more) in place through Unity Catalog foreign catalogs, with query pushdown and governance — no data movement.
note: Governed, read-only access to external data via Unity Catalog foreign catalogs. Introduced in Public Preview in 2023; the overview doc doesn't restate a single GA date.

what: The ODBC driver for connecting BI tools and applications to Databricks SQL warehouses and clusters.
note: Databricks renamed the driver in February 2026. Existing DSNs and code keep working while the legacy Simba-branded driver stays installed; Databricks stopped shipping new Simba versions but supports existing ones for two years. Same driver, new name.

what: Open protocol and Databricks service for securely sharing data and AI assets across organizations and platforms, with or without Databricks.
note: The June 2026 release notes state "Delta Sharing is now OpenSharing." The rename coincided with releasing OpenSharing as an open standard (GitHub, opensharing.io), so it is a rebrand tied to a broader open-source push rather than a cosmetic change. Delta Sharing was first announced in 2021.

what: The IDE for developing Lakeflow (Spark Declarative) pipelines — code-first, folder-based, with selective execution, data previews, and pipeline graphs on one surface.
note: The September 2025 release notes introduced it as "the Lakeflow Pipelines Editor (previously called the multi-file editor)"; the doc URL still ends in /multi-file-editor. Reached Public Preview September 2025 and GA May 2026.

name: Hive metastore
what: The per-workspace metastore that catalogs tables and views within a single workspace, predating Unity Catalog.
note: Docs state "The per-workspace Hive metastore is a legacy feature" and recommend migrating tables and workloads to Unity Catalog, which adds cross-workspace access, lineage, auditing, and access control. Databricks has positioned it as legacy since Unity Catalog's GA; the concrete account-level phase-out landed December 2025 (accounts created after December 18, 2025 have no Hive metastore access by default, though admins can still enable it per workspace). Tables remain reachable under the hive_metastore catalog namespace.

name: Lakebase
what: A fully managed, serverless Postgres (OLTP) database integrated into the Databricks platform, for building real-time transactional apps alongside lakehouse data.
note: Brings transactional (OLTP) workloads to the lakehouse, with automatic scaling, instant branching, and native Unity Catalog integration. Entered Public Preview in mid-2025; Lakebase Autoscaling and Lakebase Provisioned were unified at GA. The Compute section's "OLTP Database" tab was renamed "Lakebase Postgres" in August 2025.

name: Lakeflow Designer
what: A visual, no-code, AI-native experience for building ETL pipelines with a drag-and-drop canvas and natural language, backed by production-ready Lakeflow pipeline code.
note: Generates governed, production-ready pipeline code from visual "data preps," so prototypes move to production without a rewrite. Entered Public Preview April 2026 and reached GA June 2026.

name: Attribute-based access control (ABAC)
what: Unity Catalog access-control model that grants or restricts access by evaluating governed tags (attributes) on securables, so one policy on a catalog or schema applies to everything beneath it.
note: Supports row filters and column masks that follow governed tags across a catalog or schema instead of per-table grants. Entered Public Preview November 2025 and reached GA April 2026; GRANT policies remain in Beta.

name: Unity Catalog managed Iceberg tables
what: Apache Iceberg tables created and fully managed by Unity Catalog, readable and writable by external Iceberg engines through the Unity Catalog Iceberg REST Catalog API.
note: Managed Iceberg tables get Databricks platform features (liquid clustering, predictive optimization, materialized views) while external engines like Spark, Flink, and Trino read/write them via the Iceberg REST Catalog with credential vending. Part of Databricks' Iceberg interoperability push.

name: Serverless workspaces
what: A workspace preconfigured with serverless compute and default storage — a fully managed SaaS experience with no compute or storage running in your own cloud account.
note: Serverless compute and default storage live in the Databricks account, not the customer's cloud account, so cloud credits/discounts don't apply and billing runs through Databricks. Entered Public Preview August 2025 and reached GA January 2026.
